headline: GOLD RETREATS TO $4,736 — Down 10% From January Record as Strait of Hormuz Inflation Fears Persist
snippet: Gold slipped to $4,736/oz Thursday morning, giving back gains as elevated inflation risk from the ongoing Strait of Hormuz blockade continued to weigh on sentiment and raise central bank rate-hike speculation. The metal sits roughly 10% below its January 28 all-time high of $5,589.

headline: SILVER HITS 10-YEAR HIGH — 150% Surge Over Past Year Driven by Solar Panel Demand and Safe-Haven Flows
snippet: Silver surged more than 150% over the past year to its highest level in over a decade, powered by industrial consumption in solar panels and healthcare devices alongside traditional safe-haven buying. Analysts note silver's sharper swings relative to gold are amplified by its dual industrial-monetary demand profile.

headline: COPPER BACK NEAR $6.10/LB — Two-Month High as US-Iran Diplomacy Revives Demand Outlook
snippet: Copper futures climbed toward $6.10 per pound, fully recovering losses since the start of the Middle East conflict, as growing optimism around US-Iran diplomatic progress reduced risk premiums. AI data center build-outs and the global defense spending surge continue to underpin structural demand expectations.

headline: MAADEN PULLS BACK FROM GLOBAL MINING BUYOUTS — Saudi Giant Pivots Strategy Toward Domestic Development
snippet: Saudi Arabia's Ma'aden is shifting away from the aggressive cross-border acquisition strategy it had signalled earlier this year, according to Bloomberg, refocusing capital on domestic development projects. The move removes one of the most anticipated potential acquirers from the global mining M&A landscape.

headline: G MINING ACQUIRES G2 GOLDFIELDS FOR $2.2B — Guyana Combination Would Produce 500,000 oz/yr
snippet: G Mining Ventures Corp. agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields Inc. in an all-share deal valued at approximately $2.2 billion, consolidating two adjacent projects in Guyana into a combined asset capable of producing over 500,000 ounces of gold annually — a landmark validation of junior exploration success in the region.

headline: USA RARE EARTH SURGES ON $2.8B BRAZIL DEAL — Only Non-China Source of All 4 Magnetic Rare Earths
snippet: USA Rare Earth's acquisition of Brazil's Serra Verde for ~$2.8 billion gives the company control of the only producer outside Asia capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths at scale — a direct challenge to China's near-total grip on rare earth refining. The deal is a strategic win for allied supply chain resilience.

headline: ARGENTINA LITHIUM SIGNS $100M FRAMEWORK WITH CHINA'S LANSHEN for Rincon West DLE Brine Project
snippet: Argentina Lithium & Energy (TSX-V: LIT) signed a non-binding $100M heads of terms with Xi'an Lanshen New Material Technology, establishing a staged pathway for financing and commercializing the Rincon West brine project in Salta Province using Lanshen's Direct Lithium Extraction technology.

headline: LITHIUM CARBONATE UP 40% YEAR-TO-DATE IN CHINA — Zijin Mining Enters Large-Scale Delivery Phase
snippet: Lithium carbonate prices in China surged 40% year-to-date to approach CNY 160,000 per tonne as Zijin Mining ramps its lithium business into a large-scale commercial delivery phase — a signal that major producers see the current recovery as durable, not a head-fake.

headline: CHINA DEVOURED 790+ TONS OF SILVER IN EARLY 2026 — Solar Boom Is Creating a Structural Demand Gap
snippet: GoldSeek's weekly SWOT analysis highlights China's 790-plus tonne silver import surge in early 2026 — fuelled by record solar panel production requiring silver paste for electrical contacts — as a structural demand driver that commodity traders have yet to fully price into forward curves.

headline: 3 GOLD MINERS STILL TRADING AT BARGAIN MULTIPLES — Record Margins Haven't Moved the Needle on Valuations
snippet: With Newmont at just 12x forward earnings despite a 98% jump in 2025 EPS and gold near $4,700, analysts argue the equities haven't priced in the metal's run — creating a compelling asymmetric setup for investors who see margins staying elevated even if bullion consolidates.

headline: CONTANGO OUTLINES $63M DRILL BUDGET ACROSS ALASKA AND NORTHERN BC — One of Largest Junior Programs This Cycle
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold Inc. announced a $63 million exploration program across its consolidated portfolio of advanced gold and critical metals projects in Alaska and Northern British Columbia, one of the most ambitious junior-tier drill budgets announced in the region during the current commodity cycle.
